programmes.show_description: A playboy astrologer has to prove his alibi for a years-old robbery. With Mary Millington and Diana Dors. Contains sexual scenes and 70s social attitudes.
1/8. Suffolk boatyard specialists Mary and Simon's dream is a futuristic aquatic pod. They're experts in their aquatic field, but have they taken on more than they can handle?
2/8. Alan and Val put their savings on the line for a high-tech boat to help them spend time with their family. But first they need to learn how to live on the water.

8/35. Charlie and Flo are in Kingswinford to create a Mediterranean family garden that conjures up magical honeymoon memories for Natalie and Matthew.
Tom looks at Anglo-Indian cuisine, making oven pakora and chicken tikka. 7/10
Scottish-born actor Alan Cumming explores the picturesque Outer Hebrides of Scotland, touring the Western Isles from Barra to Stornoway on Lewis, Eriskay and the Callanish stones.
Ronnie Barker stars as General Futtock, who has a motley group of guests stay at his country estate - and chaos ensues. Contains 70s social attitudes.
Action. Would-be journalist Sonya Winter investigates the Assassination Bureau, a mysterious organization. Ollie Reed and Diana Rigg star.
In 1944 France, a group of escaped American military prisoners volunteer to steal a German V2 rocket warhead for the Allies. Wartime action starring Bo Svenson.
Bawdy seventies sketch comedy film starring a bevy of British talent from Bruce Forsyth and Harry Secombe to Joan Simms and June Whitfield. Contains 70s social attitudes.
Stories of people who have experienced infidelity, the reasons why people stray outside their relationship and if a relationship can ever survive.
Helen Mirren stars as a sex worker who begins an affair with a shady colleague at their high-class London cabaret. Can their relationship survive? Contains sexual scenes.
Comedy. Sid South is a plumber with gambling debts. So he hatches a plan to nab a Picasso. Will the plan work or will his womanising get in the way? Contains sex scenes.
3/8. Seasoned floating homeowners Pat and Sue plan to spend their retirement on a state-of-the-art Dutch barge. But will their expensive tastes sink the project?
4/8. In Surrey, Linda and Mark need an upgrade on their cramped floating home and Linda's doing it by herself. Is passion enough to get the job done?
5/8. In Kent, metal worker John tackles a cut-price riverboat restoration using ingenious workarounds. Can he make a home of his own?

9/35. Charlie and Lee head to Tadley to bring bright colour and 'wow factor' to the couple's garden, all for £4,000.
1/3. Alan Titchmarsh and his team head to Banbury to surprise young carers who help look after their mother, and transform their indoor and outdoor spaces.
6/8. An art forger's work is up for auction. Plus: Leeds football programme from 1904, a taxidermy unicorn and a set of drums from the 1970s.
Relive the glory days of good clean Hi-De-Hi style fun with this oral history of Britain's holiday camps. Hello Campers!
6/20. An antiques expert, a market trader and an upcycler battle it out to make the most money. Roo Irvine, Gary Barton and Kevin Paxton head to St Andrews.
3/8. Revealing the shocking sugar content of some smoothies. What is meant by freshly-baked by supermarket breads. Comparing fresh and frozen food.
1/8. Suffolk boatyard specialists Mary and Simon's dream is a futuristic aquatic pod. They're experts in their aquatic field, but have they taken on more than they can handle?
6/8. An art forger's work is up for auction. Plus: Leeds football programme from 1904, a taxidermy unicorn and a set of drums from the 1970s.
Four children compete in two crafty upcycling challenges, working with recycled wood. Only two will make it through to the semi-finals of 'Crafty Champions'
In this episode, Tonia Buxton pays a visit to Greece's capital Athens. She cooks avgolemono soup, which is a filling chicken and rice dish, and souvlaki - Greece's favourite fast food.(S1/E8)
3/8. Revealing the shocking sugar content of some smoothies. What is meant by freshly-baked by supermarket breads. Comparing fresh and frozen food.
Stories of people who have experienced infidelity, the reasons why people stray outside their relationship and if a relationship can ever survive.
2/6. Clips, jokes, and random points fly, as Martine & Mischa's teams battle through cheeky Deaf panel mayhem.
Dave makes room in a house in St Leonards for a new modular staircase. Last in series. 20/20
Portrait artist Alan Street is employed by the Soho Swingers Club to paint the hostesses. Whilst he relishes his new job his girlfriend is less than impressed. Contains nudity.
